title: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno
display_title: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno
wikitext:
[]: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Feb 04, 2021 5:18 am Two animated music videos were produced for the song, depicting scenes from Evangelion. The first was released at the time of the single's release, and featured scenes of Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone, directed by the film's chief director Hideaki Anno.[16] This music video won a Japanese MTV Video Music Award for the best video used for a film.

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by Mr. M » Thu Feb 04, 2021 5:32 am I think it refers to Evangelion 1.0 movie, not BW music video.

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Feb 04, 2021 5:36 am Yeah I also wondered about that once. I have no idea what they refer to. :(

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Thu Feb 04, 2021 6:50 am Huh, interesting. It was even listed in the MTV Video Music Awards Japan 2008 winners list: ■最優秀映画ビデオ賞 アーティスト/タイトル：宇多田ヒカル「"Beautiful World" from 「ヱヴァンゲリオン新劇場版：序」 Dir: 庵野秀明 (■ Best Movie Video Award Artist / Title: Hikaru Utada "" Beautiful World "from" Evangelion New Theatrical Version: Introduction " Dir: Hideaki Anno )

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Feb 04, 2021 7:39 am http://www.khara.co.jp/history-en/ khara's website only mention the Tsurumaki video

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by Fahrenberg_39 » Wed Jul 21, 2021 4:38 pm Hmm I'm not really sure if this is the one, but I found a version that looks fairly "old" and isn't the Tsurumaki version, it even has the usual info that appears on MTVS music videos It was uploaded all the way back to October 2012 by a Facebook user named "Omar Delfin Cardenas Coricaza" I'll leave the links below and maybe one of us can recognize if it's legitimately the Anno one or another thing If it is the one we're looking for, the best option would be to recreate it with the 1.11 BD footage, since the video quality is pretty bad The video: https://www.facebook.com/omardelfin.car ... 439312287/ The user: https://www.facebook.com/omardelfin.cardenascoricaza

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Jul 22, 2021 6:18 pm This is strangely similar to the only other beautiful world music video on Youtube (that isn't the Tsurumaki video) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4jatk9Dvjd8 At certain points in the video it aligns exactly with the Facebook video, but why are there two versions of the same thing, with slightly different scenes? The only way to get access to the true video would be if someone owns the Evangelion 1.0 limited edition disc, which I believe contains the music videos.

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by FryMeTuhDehMuhn » Fri Jul 23, 2021 11:31 am dzzthink wrote: The only way to get access to the true video would be if someone owns the Evangelion 1.0 limited edition disc, which I believe contains the music videos.

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jul 23, 2021 12:26 pm Mine (1.11 UK) has "Angel of Doom Promotional Music Video", which is nothing to do with Beautiful World, of course; and three trailers which play Beautiful World for 1'36", ending with the release details in different places - these are not full-on music videos.

[]: Re: Original Beautiful World music video directed by Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by FryMeTuhDehMuhn » Fri Jul 23, 2021 1:17 pm Darn, you're correct. I'm sorry, I only have the "Angel of Doom" music video as well. I do wonder though if maybe there are two music videos as they were advertising different aspects of the film? 3.0+1.0's sort of done this too, where they've had A and B trailers which advertise different aspects of the film, so maybe 1.0 did this with music videos instead.
